questions.question: What percentage of all road accidents are caused by human error?
questions.explanation: Over 90% of road accidents are attributed to human error, highlighting the critical importance of defensive driving training.

questions.question: According to the Smith System, how far ahead should a driver look at 60 km/h?
questions.explanation: The Smith System recommends looking 12-15 seconds ahead, which translates to approximately 250m at 60 km/h.

questions.question: How often should a professional driver check their mirrors?
questions.explanation: Professional drivers should check mirrors every 5-8 seconds to maintain full situational awareness.

questions.question: What is the minimum following gap recommended on dry roads at normal speeds?
questions.explanation: A 3-second following gap is the minimum recommended on dry roads to allow adequate reaction and braking time.

questions.question: After how many consecutive hours awake does driver impairment equal a 0.05% BAC?
questions.explanation: After 17 hours awake, driver impairment is equivalent to a blood alcohol concentration of 0.05%.

questions.question: What does the 'P' in the POWER check stand for?
questions.explanation: In the POWER pre-trip inspection, P stands for Protection (seatbelts, first aid, safety equipment).

questions.question: What is the minimum legal tyre tread depth in most jurisdictions?
questions.explanation: The minimum legal tread depth in most jurisdictions is 1.6mm, though professional fleets should maintain higher standards.

questions.question: At what speed does a pedestrian struck have approximately a 90% survival chance?
questions.explanation: At 50 km/h, a struck pedestrian has approximately a 90% survival chance; this drops dramatically at higher speeds.

questions.question: What does 'SMIDSY' stand for in road safety terminology?
options: Sorry Mate I Didn't See You
Safety Means I Drive Smartly Yearly
Signal Mirrors Indicate Direction Safely Yesterday
Slow Moving In Dangerous Street Y-junction
questions.explanation: SMIDSY is an acronym for 'Sorry Mate I Didn't See You' - a common phrase after collisions involving vulnerable road users.

questions.question: Which of the following is a STATIC hazard?
options: A child running into the road
A pothole in the road surface
questions.explanation: Static hazards are fixed objects or conditions like potholes, parked vehicles, or road signs that don't move.

questions.question: What is the recommended clearance when passing parked vehicles in urban areas?
questions.explanation: A 1-metre clearance is recommended when passing parked vehicles to protect against suddenly opened doors or pedestrians.

questions.question: How much does seatbelt use reduce fatality risk for rear-seat passengers in serious crashes?
questions.explanation: Seatbelt use reduces fatality risk for rear-seat passengers by up to 75% in serious crashes.

questions.question: What is the primary cause of target fixation while driving?
options: Staring at a hazard rather than scanning away from it
questions.explanation: Target fixation occurs when a driver stares at a hazard instead of looking where they want to go, often causing them to steer toward it.

questions.question: In the HALT check before driving, what does the 'H' stand for?
questions.explanation: HALT stands for Hungry, Angry, Late, Tired - conditions that impair driving performance and should be addressed before driving.

questions.question: What is the professional standard for alcohol consumption before driving?
options: One beer or glass of wine only
questions.explanation: Professional drivers should maintain zero tolerance for alcohol consumption before driving.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT part of the 'W' (Wheels & Tyres) check in POWER?
questions.explanation: Windshield wipers are part of the 'E' (Electrics & Extras) check, not the 'W' (Wheels & Tyres) check.

questions.question: What does an amber traffic signal mean?
options: Speed up to clear the junction
Prepare to stop unless doing so would be unsafe
questions.explanation: An amber light means prepare to stop, unless you are so close to the junction that stopping would be unsafe.

questions.question: How many global road deaths occur per day according to WHO data?
questions.explanation: According to WHO data, approximately 3,700 people die on roads globally every day.

questions.question: Which of the following is a DYNAMIC hazard?
options: A sharp bend in the road
A cyclist overtaking on the left
A traffic sign warning of roadworks
questions.explanation: Dynamic hazards are moving or changing hazards like other vehicles, pedestrians, or cyclists.

questions.question: What is the recommended minimum space cushion to the front of your vehicle?
questions.explanation: A 3-second following distance provides the minimum safe space cushion to the front of your vehicle.
